item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i can't believe i novoted this when i originally read it in march. absolutely a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I worked out a really cool easter egg for an SCP I'm working on and it would have been perfect to have this slot. Had to be this one that was taken. Cool SCP though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also the fact that, in our timeline, Henry Clay was not murdered. If more had been done, in the narrative of the article, to establish that we are looking at an alternate reality, it would be more interesting if and when one either looks for the historical figures mentioned, or someone who knows the history reads it for the first time. I feel that it would help the article be more choherent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really feels like it's trying the same thing that SCP-2158 (The Gun That Never Misses) is, nearly beat-for-beat, just without the benefit of brevity. Sorry, but it's a downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Thoughts!
- Thought.

I hate to be the fly in this article’s ointment, but: while the idea of a retrocausal pistol is mildly interesting, I found the setup to be a little janky.

Four characters with no established connection are introduced between the second paragraph of the conprocs and the first of the description. A little too brisk for me!

I think it would flow better if the Lockley/Clay section were framed as an update in response to the ASCI document. It signals to the reader that there’s a good reason for these conprocs that will be revealed later. But as it stands, having that many names unveiled at once left me a bit breathless.

I also tripped over this -

SCP-5908 was delivered six days after Andrew Jackson's death and remained unused until recovered by the Foundation.

because I’ve been conditioned to interpret that as “delivered to the Foundation”. (You hadn’t introduced the warehouse yet!) For that reason, I think Description/Discovery would work better if woven into a single section.

In a nutshell, I think this is an issue of clarity. There’s quite a few things going on in your conprocs and description. None are hard to understand by themselves. But I feel that they don’t gel together harmoniously to set up the backstory in a satisfying way.

It’s a novote from me. Sorry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great read, lots of fun. Imagine the sheer WTF that the ASCI folks experienced.

item.title: Missing Bullets
item.content:encoded:
Looks like the Foundation made a whoopsie! Andrew Jackson despite all his faults was one badass mofo, that quote at the end is a real one and the inspiration for this article. I hope y'all enjoy it!

I'd like to thank my critters, but I, unfortunately, let some time pass before filling this in. If you gave a peek before I posted it just give me a poke and I'll add you in.
